findings.evidence: `rg` shows the general API already exists: `DiagonalizableGroupBaseChange.lean:72 def baseChangePointsMulEquiv : WithConv (K ⊗[k] MonoidAlgebra k G →ₐ[K] A) ≃* (G →* Aˣ)`, with generator and naturality lemmas at lines 92, 113, and 124, while this file repeats the same base-change composition at lines 67-72 and repeats the special generator/naturality lemmas at lines 77-118.
findings.fix: Import/use `TauCeti.Algebra.AlgebraicGroup.DiagonalizableGroupBaseChange` and define the roots-of-unity equivalence and its simp lemmas as the specialization of `DiagonalizableGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv` at `G := Multiplicative (ZMod n)` composed with the cyclic-character-to-`rootsOfUnity` equivalence; expose/refactor that latter equivalence if needed.
findings.issue: `RootsOfUnityGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv` reproves the base-changed diagonalizable-points calculation for the special group `Multiplicative (ZMod n)` rather than using the general `DiagonalizableGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv`.

summary: The new roots-of-unity base-change API duplicates the already-existing diagonalizable-group base-change API instead of deriving the specialization from it.
